                    await _asyncio.sleep(wait)
                else:
                    raise
        raise RuntimeError("LLM call failed after 5 attempts")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # PROMPT CONSTRUCTION
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _build_system_prompt(self, description: str, project_id: str) -> str:
        schema_text = build_compact_schema_text(self.models, self.topology_yaml or "")

        return f"""You are a Worker Agent executing a cyber-exercise creation task.
You must create ALL artifacts required to fully implement the exercise described below.

YOUR ASSIGNMENT:
{description}

PROJECT ID (injected automatically — do NOT pass it as a tool argument):
{project_id}

{schema_text}

FIELD POPULATION RULES:
- Fields marked with * are REQUIRED — you must provide a value.
- REFERENCE fields (marked with "-> TargetClass") point to other artifacts.
  Use set_reference or append_to_collection for these — NEVER put them in batch_set_fields.
- Enum fields must use one of the listed values.
- Populate ALL scalar fields (name, description, ip, cidr, etc.) via batch_set_fields.
- Create artifacts following the phase order (phase 0 first, then phase 1, etc.).

AVAILABLE TOOLS:

  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  🔧 ARTIFACT MANAGEMENT TOOLS
  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  query_artifacts(model_name)
    → List existing artifacts of a type.
    → Returns: [{{"artifact_id": "...", "data": {{...}}}}]

  get_artifact(artifact_id)
    → Inspect an existing artifact.
    → Returns: {{"success": true, "artifact": {{...}}}}

  get_model_schema(model_name)
    → Get detailed field information for a model (use to discover field names).
    → Returns: {{"success": true, "schema": {{...}}}}

  create_artifact(model_name, common_name?)
    → Create a new empty artifact shell.
    → Parameters: model_name (string), common_name (optional short label)
    → Returns: {{"success": true, "artifact_id": "<UUID>"}}
    → ⚠️  The returned artifact_id UUID is what you use in ALL subsequent calls.

  batch_set_fields(artifact_id, fields_json)
    → Set SCALAR (primitive) fields: strings, numbers, booleans, enums.
    → Parameters:
        • artifact_id: UUID from create_artifact
        • fields_json: JSON string with field→value pairs (scalars only)
    → ❌ NEVER put reference fields here. Reference fields (pointing to other
         artifacts) MUST use set_reference or append_to_collection.
    → Returns: {{"success": true}} or {{"success": false, "error": "..."}}

  set_reference(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value)
    → Link a single-valued reference field to another artifact.
    → Parameters:
        • artifact_id: UUID of the artifact being modified
        • field_name: the reference field name (from get_model_schema)
        • reference_value: UUID of the artifact to reference
    → Returns: {{"success": true}}

  append_to_collection(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value)
    → Append to a list/collection reference field.
    → Parameters same as set_reference.
    → Returns: {{"success": true}}

  validate_artifact(artifact_id)
    → Validate against the metamodel schema.
    → Returns: {{"valid": true}} or {{"valid": false, "errors": [...]}}

  commit_artifact(artifact_id)
    → Finalize the artifact. Call ONLY after validate returns valid=true.
    → Returns: {{"success": true}}

  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  ✅ COMPLETION SIGNALS
  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  finish()
    → Signal that ALL artifacts are created, validated, and committed.
    → Call this as your LAST action when the full exercise is done.

  fail(reason)
    → Signal that you cannot complete the task. Use only if truly stuck.

RULES:
  1. Use get_model_schema(model_name) to discover field names and types before
     creating an artifact if you are unsure of its fields.
  2. Call create_artifact once per artifact to get its UUID.
  3. Call batch_set_fields with ALL scalar fields in a single call per artifact.
  4. Use set_reference / append_to_collection for any field that points to another
     artifact — NEVER pass artifact data as a dict inside batch_set_fields.
  5. Call validate_artifact before commit_artifact.
  6. Fix validation errors before committing.
  7. Call commit_artifact only after validation passes.
  8. When all artifacts are committed, call finish().
  9. NEVER repeat a tool call with the exact same arguments — reuse results from history.
  10. Do NOT pass project_id as a tool argument — it is injected automatically.
  11. Build artifacts bottom-up: create dependencies (Networks, Hardware) before
      the artifacts that reference them (Nodes, VMs).

OUTPUT FORMAT — respond with ONLY valid JSON, no prose, no markdown:
{{"tool": "tool_name_here", "args": {{"arg1": "value1"}}}}

Examples:
  {{"tool": "get_model_schema", "args": {{"model_name": "Node"}}}}
  {{"tool": "create_artifact", "args": {{"model_name": "Network", "common_name": "hq_lan"}}}}
  {{"tool": "batch_set_fields", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>", "fields_json": "{{\\"name\\": \\"HQ LAN\\", \\"cidr\\": \\"10.0.0.0/24\\"}}"}}}}
  {{"tool": "set_reference", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>", "field_name": "topology", "reference_value": "<topology_uuid>"}}}}
  {{"tool": "validate_artifact", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>"}}}}
  {{"tool": "commit_artifact", "args": {{"artifact_id": "<uuid>"}}}}
  {{"tool": "finish", "args": {{}}}}
"""

    def _maybe_compress_history(self, tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Rolling compression of tool history to stay within context limits."""
        if len(tool_history) <= CONTEXT_COMPRESS_THRESHOLD:
            return tool_history

        to_compress = tool_history[:-CONTEXT_KEEP_RECENT]
        remaining = tool_history[-CONTEXT_KEEP_RECENT:]

        lines: List[str] = []
        create_ids: List[str] = []
        fields_set: List[str] = []
        validations_passed = 0
        failures = 0

        for entry in to_compress:
            tool_name = entry.get("tool")
            res = entry.get("result", {})
            success = entry.get("success", True)
            
            if tool_name == "create_artifact" and success:
                aid = res.get("artifact_id", "?")
                create_ids.append(aid)
            elif tool_name == "batch_set_fields" and success:
                try:
                    fkeys = list(json.loads(entry.get("args", {}).get("fields_json", "{}")).keys())
                    fields_set.extend(fkeys)
                except Exception:
                    pass
            elif tool_name == "validate_artifact":
                if res.get("valid"):
                    validations_passed += 1
            if not success:
                failures += 1

        if create_ids:
            lines.append(f"Created artifact(s): {', '.join(create_ids)}")
        if fields_set:
            unique_fields = list(dict.fromkeys(fields_set))
            lines.append(f"Fields set: {', '.join(unique_fields[:20])}")
        if validations_passed:
            lines.append(f"Validation passed {validations_passed} time(s)")
        if failures:
            lines.append(f"Tool failures encountered: {failures}")

        summary_chunk = f"[Steps 1-{len(to_compress)} compressed] " + "; ".join(lines) + "."
        if self.compressed_history_summary:
            self.compressed_history_summary += "\n" + summary_chunk
        else:
            self.compressed_history_summary = summary_chunk
            
        return remaining

    def _build_messages(
        self,
        system_prompt: str,
        tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]],
        consecutive_failures: List[Dict[str, Any]],
    ) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
        messages: List[Dict[str, str]] = [{"role": "system", "content": system_prompt}]

        if self.compressed_history_summary:
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": f"PROGRESS SUMMARY (earlier steps):\n{self.compressed_history_summary}"})

        for entry in tool_history:
            messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": json.dumps({"tool": entry["tool"], "args": entry["args"]})})
            obs = json.dumps(entry["result"], default=str)
            if len(obs) > 4000:
                obs = obs[:4000] + "… [truncated]"
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": f"OBSERVATION: {obs}"})

        if len(consecutive_failures) >= 2:
            error_lines = "\n".join(
                f"  • {e['tool']}: {e['result'].get('error', 'failed')}"
                for e in consecutive_failures
            )
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": (
                f"⚠️  REPEATED TOOL FAILURES ({len(consecutive_failures)} in a row):\n"
                f"{error_lines}\n\n"
                "You must change your approach. Before your next action:\n"
                "1. State the ROOT CAUSE of these failures\n"
                "2. Describe your CORRECTED PLAN\n"
                "3. Then provide the corrected JSON action"
            )})
        elif len(consecutive_failures) == 1:
            e = consecutive_failures[0]
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": (
                f"The previous {e['tool']} call failed: {e['result'].get('error', 'unknown error')}\n"
                "Briefly reason about what went wrong, then provide a corrected JSON action."
            )})

        messages.append({"role": "user", "content": "What is your next action? Respond with JSON only."})
        return messages

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # JSON PARSER (same balanced-brace approach as WorkerAgent)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _parse_action(self, content: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        content = content.strip()
        try:
            return json.loads(content)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            pass
        code_block = re.search(r"```(?:json)?\s*([\s\S]*?)\s*```", content)
        if code_block:
            try:
                return json.loads(code_block.group(1))
            except json.JSONDecodeError:
                pass
        for start in range(len(content)):
            if content[start] != "{":
                continue
            depth, in_string, escape_next = 0, False, False
            for end in range(start, len(content)):
                ch = content[end]
                if escape_next:
                    escape_next = False
                    continue
                if ch == "\\" and in_string:
                    escape_next = True
                    continue
                if ch == '"':
                    in_string = not in_string
                    continue
                if in_string:
                    continue
                if ch == "{":
                    depth += 1
                elif ch == "}":
                    depth -= 1
                    if depth == 0:
                        try:
                            return json.loads(content[start:end + 1])
                        except json.JSONDecodeError:
                            break
        return None

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # MAIN LOOP
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    async def generate(
        self, description: str, project_id: str = "", log_callback: Optional[callable] = None
    ) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], List[Dict[str, Any]]]:
        start_time = time.perf_counter()
        model_name = resolve_baseline_model()

        self.logs.append({
            "event": "start",
            "model": model_name,
            "mcp_url": self.mcp_server_url,
            "project_id": project_id,
            "description_preview": description[:200],
        })
        if log_callback:
            log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)

        transport = StreamableHttpTransport(url=self.mcp_server_url)
        async with Client(transport=transport) as mcp:
            mcp_tools = await mcp.list_tools()
            tool_names = [t.name for t in mcp_tools]
            self.logs.append({"event": "tools_discovered", "tools": tool_names})
            if log_callback:
                log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
            print(f"      [react_agent] discovered {len(tool_names)} MCP tools", flush=True)

            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt(description, project_id)
            tool_history: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

            for step in range(MAX_STEPS):
                self.metrics["iterations"] += 1
                
                # Compress history if it exceeds threshold
                tool_history = self._maybe_compress_history(tool_history)

                # Compute consecutive tail failures for error injection
                consecutive_failures: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
                for entry in reversed(tool_history):
                    if not entry.get("success", True):
                        consecutive_failures.insert(0, entry)
                    else:
                        break

                if len(consecutive_failures) >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES:
                    self.logs.append({"event": "fatal_failures", "step": step, "count": len(consecutive_failures)})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: EXCEEDED {MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES} CONSECUTIVE FAILURES. Stopping.", flush=True)
                    break

                messages = self._build_messages(system_prompt, tool_history, consecutive_failures)
                raw = await self._call_llm(messages)
                action = self._parse_action(raw)

                if action is None:
                    self.logs.append({"event": "parse_failure", "step": step, "raw_preview": raw[:300]})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: could not parse JSON action", flush=True)
                    # Inject parse failure feedback and continue
                    tool_history.append({
                        "tool": "_parse_error",
                        "args": {},
                        "result": {"error": f"Your response was not valid JSON. Raw: {raw[:200]}"},
                        "success": False,
                    })
                    continue

                tool_name = action.get("tool", "")
                tool_args = action.get("args", {})

                # Termination signals
                if tool_name == "finish":
                    self.logs.append({"event": "finish", "step": step})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: finish() called", flush=True)
                    break
                if tool_name == "fail":
                    reason = tool_args.get("reason", "no reason given")
                    self.logs.append({"event": "fail", "step": step, "reason": reason})
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: fail({reason})", flush=True)
                    break

                # Inject project_id for artifact tools that accept it
                tool_args["project_id"] = project_id

                try:
                    self.metrics["tool_calls"] += 1
                    _raw = await mcp.call_tool(tool_name, tool_args)

                    if hasattr(_raw, "structured_content") and _raw.structured_content is not None:
                        observation = _raw.structured_content
                    elif hasattr(_raw, "content") and _raw.content:
                        texts = [c.text for c in _raw.content if hasattr(c, "text")]
                        try:
                            observation = json.loads(texts[0]) if texts else {}
                        except Exception:
                            observation = texts[0] if texts else str(_raw)
                    else:
                        observation = str(_raw)

                    success = observation.get("success", True) if isinstance(observation, dict) else True
                    if not success:
                        self.metrics["tool_failures"] += 1

                    self.logs.append({
                        "event": "tool_call", "step": step, "tool": tool_name,
                        "args_preview": json.dumps(tool_args)[:300],
                        "success": success,
                        "observation_preview": json.dumps(observation, default=str)[:600],
                    })
                    if log_callback:
                        log_callback(self.logs, self.metrics)
                    obs_preview = json.dumps(observation, default=str)
                    if len(obs_preview) > 200:
                        obs_preview = obs_preview[:200] + "..."
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: {tool_name} → success={success} | Result: {obs_preview}", flush=True)
                    tool_history.append({"tool": tool_name, "args": tool_args, "result": observation, "success": success})

                except Exception as exc:
                    self.metrics["tool_failures"] += 1
                    err_msg = str(exc)
                    self.logs.append({
                        "event": "tool_error", "step": step, "tool": tool_name,
                        "error": err_msg, "error_class": classify_error(err_msg),
                    })
                    print(f"      [react_agent] step {step}: {tool_name} ERROR: {err_msg[:100]}", flush=True)
                    tool_history.append({"tool": tool_name, "args": tool_args, "result": {"error": err_msg}, "success": False})

content.text:
SHELL := /bin/bash
DC = docker compose -f docker-compose.yml
PYTHON ?= python3

# ==============================================================================
# Benchmark variables — all can be overridden on the command line
# ==============================================================================
#
# Auth (auto-read from .env, no manual token needed in normal use):
#   AUTH_TOKEN              pre-obtained JWT (optional — login is automatic)
#   BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL    admin e-mail  (read from .env by default)
#   BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD admin password (read from .env by default)
#
# Study scope:
#   SUITE       baselines | ablation | full | all  (default: baselines)
#   DATASET     path to a single ex_*.json; empty = every ex_*.json
#   RUN_NAME    fixed directory name for idempotent / resumable runs
#
# Judge management (for existing runs, no re-creation needed):
#   REJUDGE=1       run only NEW judges not yet in stored results
#   REAGGREGATE=1   recompute scores/kappa offline (no LLM calls)
#   INCLUDE_JUDGES  comma-separated judge names to use in reaggregation
#
# Quick reference:
#   make benchmark-study                          # baselines suite, all datasets
#   make benchmark-study SUITE=ablation           # ablation suite
#   make benchmark-study SUITE=all RUN_NAME=pub   # full study, resumable
#   make benchmark-study DATASET=evaluation/datasets/ex_01_audit.json RUN_NAME=smoke
#   make benchmark-comparative RUN_NAME=smoke     # single-dataset, host-side
#   make benchmark-rejudge    RUN_NAME=smoke       # add new judges to existing run
#   make benchmark-reaggregate RUN_NAME=smoke      # offline score recompute
#   make benchmark-report     RUN_DIR=evaluation/results/smoke

BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR    ?= evaluation/results
BENCHMARK_INPUT         ?= evaluation/datasets/research_input.json
SUITE                   ?= baselines
RUN_NAME                ?=
DATASET                 ?=
REJUDGE                 ?=
REAGGREGATE             ?=
ONLY_EVALUATE           ?=
ONLY_JUDGE              ?=
SKIP_EVALUATE           ?=
FORCE_JUDGE             ?=
FORCE_CREATE            ?=
INCLUDE_JUDGES          ?=
# Host user identity — passed to docker exec so result files are user-owned
HOST_UID                := $(shell id -u)
HOST_GID                := $(shell id -g)
SCORPION_CONFIG_ID      ?= 925b3c1b-df6f-4a63-89e5-8c3bcfdc3e3f
ORCHESTRATOR_URL        ?= http://localhost:12013/api/v1
CONFIG_SERVICE_URL      ?= http://localhost:12011
EVALUATION_URL          ?= http://localhost:12015/api/v1/evaluate
AUTH_BASE_URL           ?= http://localhost:12000
AUTH_TOKEN              ?=
# Auto-read credentials from .env — no manual input required
BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL    ?= $(shell grep '^BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL=' .env 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2-)
BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD ?= $(shell grep '^BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD=' .env 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2-)

# Legacy aliases (keep old invocations working)
CONFIGURATION_ID        ?= $(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)
METAMODEL_ID            ?=
BENCHMARK_CREATE_OUTPUT ?= evaluation/results/research_created_projects.json
BENCHMARK_EVAL_OUTPUT   ?= evaluation/results/research_evaluations.json
BENCHMARK_FULL_OUTPUT   ?= evaluation/results/research_full_run.json
BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT  ?= evaluation/datasets/ex_01_audit.json
BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR ?= evaluation/results

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target strings by suite
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
_T_FULL      := FullSystem:$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)
_T_BASELINES := Baseline-Direct:direct_llm,Baseline-ReAct:react_agent,Baseline-CodeGen:code_gen
_T_ABLATION  := Ablation-NoMetamodel:ablation:no_metamodel_guide,Ablation-NoBlackboard:ablation:no_blackboard,Ablation-NoRAG:ablation:no_rag
_T_MODIFY    := ModifyVsRegen:modify

ifeq ($(SUITE),baselines)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),only-baselines)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_BASELINES)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),ablation)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_ABLATION)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),only-ablation)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_ABLATION)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),full)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),modify)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_MODIFY)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),smoke)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_MODIFY)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),all)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES),$(_T_ABLATION),$(_T_MODIFY)
else
$(error Unknown SUITE=$(SUITE). Use: baselines | only-baselines | ablation | only-ablation | modify | smoke | full | all)
endif

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared auth macro — acquires a JWT and exports it as $$TOKEN.
# Uses AUTH_TOKEN directly if provided; otherwise logs in from .env credentials.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
define _GET_TOKEN
TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
	if [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" ] || [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL or BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD not set in .env"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  Attempting auth for $(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)..."; \
	RESPONSE=$$(curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12010/api/auth/login/" \
		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}" \
		|| curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12000/api/v1/auth/login/" \
		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}"); \
	TOKEN=$$(echo "$$RESPONSE" | $(PYTHON) -c "import sys, json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('access', ''))" 2>/dev/null); \
	if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: Auth failed. Response from server:"; \
		echo "$$RESPONSE"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  Auth OK"; \
fi
endef

# Same macro for inside a running Docker container (uses internal hostnames)
define _GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER
TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
	TOKEN=$$(docker exec benchmark-env python3 -c \
		"import httpx; r=httpx.post('http://auth-service:12010/api/auth/login/', \
		    json={'email':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)','password':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)'}, \
		    timeout=20); r.raise_for_status(); print(r.json()['access'])"); \
	echo "  Auth OK inside container ($(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL))"; \
fi
endef

# Webapp-only services (auth + config + frontend — no orchestrator/artifacts)
WEBAPP_SERVICES = traefik auth-postgres auth-service config-mongo config-service webapp

.PHONY: help up up-webapp down down-webapp logs logs-webapp status clean \
        benchmark-study benchmark-comparative \
        benchmark-rejudge benchmark-reaggregate benchmark-judge \
        benchmark-report benchmark-auth-check \
        benchmark-create benchmark-evaluate benchmark-run-all \
        benchmark-baselines benchmark-run-server benchmark-run-unified

help:
	@echo "Co-Creation Framework"
	@echo "====================="
	@echo ""
	@echo "Stack management"
	@echo "----------------"
	@echo "  make up             Start full stack (all services)"
	@echo "  make up-webapp      Start minimal stack (traefik + auth + config + webapp)"
	@echo "  make down           Stop all services"
	@echo "  make logs           Tail all service logs"
	@echo "  make status         Show service status"
	@echo "  make clean          Stop all services and remove volumes"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Research benchmarks"
	@echo "-------------------"
	@echo "  make benchmark-study              Full study inside Docker (idempotent)."
	@echo "                                    Loops over all ex_*.json datasets."
	@echo "    SUITE=baselines|ablation|modify|smoke|full|all  (default: baselines)"
	@echo "    DATASET=path/to/ex_N.json          run a single dataset instead"
	@echo "    RUN_NAME=my_run                    fixed dir name for resumable runs"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make benchmark-comparative        Single-dataset run on the host."
	@echo "    BENCHMARK_INPUT=...  RUN_NAME=... SUITE=..."
	@echo "    REJUDGE=1            add new judges to an existing run"
	@echo "    REAGGREGATE=1        recompute scores offline (no LLM calls)"
	@echo "    INCLUDE_JUDGES=j1,j2 subset of judges for reaggregation"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make benchmark-rejudge   RUN_NAME=<run>   (shortcut for REJUDGE=1)"
	@echo "  make benchmark-reaggregate RUN_NAME=<run>  (shortcut for REAGGREGATE=1)"
	@echo "  make benchmark-judge     RUN_NAME=<run>   skip creation, run judge on snapshots"
	@echo "  make benchmark-report    RUN_DIR=evaluation/results/<run>"
	@echo "  make benchmark-auth-check                  verify credentials"
	@echo ""
	@echo "SUITE values: baselines | only-baselines | ablation | only-ablation | modify | smoke | full | all"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Auth: credentials are auto-read from .env (BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL / PASSWORD)."
	@echo "      Override with  AUTH_TOKEN=<jwt>  if you have a pre-obtained token."

# ==============================================================================
# Stack targets
# ==============================================================================

up:
	$(DC) up -d --build

up-webapp:
	$(DC) up -d --build $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

down:
	$(DC) down

down-webapp:
	$(DC) stop $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

logs:
	$(DC) logs -f

logs-webapp:
	$(DC) logs -f $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

status:
	$(DC) ps

clean:
	$(DC) down -v --remove-orphans

# ==============================================================================
# Benchmark: setup and auth check
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-setup:
	@echo "Ensuring benchmarking configuration exists..."
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	curl -s -X GET "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)/api/v1/configurations/$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		-H "Authorization: Bearer $$TOKEN" | grep -q "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" || \
		(echo "  Config $(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID) not found. It should be seeded automatically on config-service startup." && \
		 echo "  If you just started the services, wait a few seconds and try again." && \
		 exit 1)
	@echo "  Benchmarking configuration OK"

benchmark-auth-check:
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	echo "  Token length: $${#TOKEN}"; \
	echo "  benchmark-auth-check: OK"

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-study — main entry point (runs inside Docker, idempotent)
#
# Starts the benchmark container, then launches run_all_benchmarks.sh inside
# it with the chosen SUITE.  Each dataset gets a stable RUN_NAME so re-running
# the same command resumes from where it left off.
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-study: benchmark-setup
	@echo "Starting benchmark container (research profile)..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	echo "Running suite='$(SUITE)' inside container..."; \
	INP="$(DATASET)"; [ -z "$$INP" ] && INP="$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)"; \
	if [ -n "$$INP" ]; then \
		docker exec \
			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
			-e DATASETS_DIR="/dev/null" \
			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
			benchmark-env \
			python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
				--input "$$INP" \
				--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
				--output-dir "/app/src/evaluation/results" \
				$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
				--orchestrator-url "http://langgraph-orchestrator:12013/api/v1" \
				--config-service-url "http://config-service:12011" \
				--evaluation-url "http://evaluation-service:12015/api/v1/evaluate" \
				--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
				--token "$$TOKEN" \
				--auth-base-url "http://auth-service:12010" \
				--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
				--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
				$(if $(REJUDGE),--rejudge,) \
				$(if $(REAGGREGATE),--reaggregate,) \
				$(if $(ONLY_EVALUATE),--only-evaluate,) \
				$(if $(ONLY_JUDGE),--only-judge,) \
				$(if $(SKIP_EVALUATE),--skip-evaluate,) \
				$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
				$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
				$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",); \
	else \
		docker exec \
			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
			-e DATASETS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/datasets" \
			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
			benchmark-env \
			bash /app/src/evaluation/run_all_benchmarks.sh; \
	fi

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-comparative — single dataset, runs on host (fast iteration)
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-comparative: benchmark-setup
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(REJUDGE),--rejudge,) \
		$(if $(REAGGREGATE),--reaggregate,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-rejudge — add new judges to an existing run (no re-creation)
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously started run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-rejudge:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--rejudge \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-reaggregate — recompute scores/kappa from stored judge data (no LLM)
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously evaluated run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-reaggregate:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--reaggregate \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-judge — skip creation, run LLM-as-judge on existing snapshots
# Combines --only-evaluate --rejudge --reaggregate in one step.
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously created run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-judge:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--only-judge \
		$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-report — generate markdown/CSV report from an existing run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-report:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_DIR)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_DIR (e.g., evaluation/results/benchmark_ex_01_audit)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparison_reporter "$(RUN_DIR)"

# ==============================================================================
# Legacy / convenience targets (kept for backwards compatibility)
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-create:
	@if [ -z "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)" ] && [ -z "$(METAMODEL_ID)" ]; then \
		echo "Set CONFIGURATION_ID or METAMODEL_ID"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode create \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_CREATE_OUTPUT)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(CONFIGURATION_ID),--configuration-id "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)",) \
		$(if $(METAMODEL_ID),--metamodel-id "$(METAMODEL_ID)",)

benchmark-evaluate:
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode evaluate \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_EVAL_OUTPUT)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN"

benchmark-run-all:
	@if [ -z "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)" ] && [ -z "$(METAMODEL_ID)" ]; then \
		echo "Set CONFIGURATION_ID or METAMODEL_ID"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode both \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_FULL_OUTPUT)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(CONFIGURATION_ID),--configuration-id "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)",) \
		$(if $(METAMODEL_ID),--metamodel-id "$(METAMODEL_ID)",)

benchmark-baselines:
	@echo "Starting benchmark container (research profile)..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	RUN_TS=$$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S); \
	CONSOLE_LOG="$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)/baselines_console_$${RUN_TS}.log"; \
	mkdir -p "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)"; \
	echo "Running baselines on $(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT)  (log: $$CONSOLE_LOG)"; \
	docker exec \
		-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
		-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
		benchmark-env \
		python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
			--input "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT)" \
			--targets "$(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES)" \
			--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
			--orchestrator-url "http://langgraph-orchestrator:12013/api/v1" \
			--config-service-url "http://config-service:12011" \
			--evaluation-url "http://evaluation-service:12015/api/v1/evaluate" \
			--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			--token "$$TOKEN" \
			--auth-base-url "http://auth-service:12010" \
			--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
	2>&1 | tee "$$CONSOLE_LOG"

benchmark-run-server:
	@echo "Launching server-side benchmark auto-runner (profile: autorun)..."
	@echo "All services must already be up (make up)."
	@echo "Results -> evaluation/results/ (mounted volume)"
	docker compose --profile autorun up --build benchmark-runner

benchmark-run-unified:
	@echo "Starting benchmark container..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	docker exec \
		-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
		-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
		-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
		benchmark-env \
		python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
			--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
			--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
			--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
			$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
