truncation.content:
SHELL := /bin/bash
DC = docker compose -f docker-compose.yml
PYTHON ?= python3

# ==============================================================================
# Benchmark variables — all can be overridden on the command line
# ==============================================================================
#
# Auth (auto-read from .env, no manual token needed in normal use):
#   AUTH_TOKEN              pre-obtained JWT (optional — login is automatic)
#   BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL    admin e-mail  (read from .env by default)
#   BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD admin password (read from .env by default)
#
# Study scope:
#   SUITE       baselines | ablation | full | all  (default: baselines)
#   DATASET     path to a single ex_*.json; empty = every ex_*.json
#   RUN_NAME    fixed directory name for idempotent / resumable runs
#
# Judge management (for existing runs, no re-creation needed):
#   REJUDGE=1       run only NEW judges not yet in stored results
#   REAGGREGATE=1   recompute scores/kappa offline (no LLM calls)
#   INCLUDE_JUDGES  comma-separated judge names to use in reaggregation
#
# Quick reference:
#   make benchmark-study                          # baselines suite, all datasets
#   make benchmark-study SUITE=ablation           # ablation suite
#   make benchmark-study SUITE=all RUN_NAME=pub   # full study, resumable
#   make benchmark-study DATASET=evaluation/datasets/ex_01_audit.json RUN_NAME=smoke
#   make benchmark-comparative RUN_NAME=smoke     # single-dataset, host-side
#   make benchmark-rejudge    RUN_NAME=smoke       # add new judges to existing run
#   make benchmark-reaggregate RUN_NAME=smoke      # offline score recompute
#   make benchmark-report     RUN_DIR=evaluation/results/smoke

BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR    ?= evaluation/results
BENCHMARK_INPUT         ?= evaluation/datasets/research_input.json
SUITE                   ?= baselines
RUN_NAME                ?=
DATASET                 ?=
REJUDGE                 ?=
REAGGREGATE             ?=
ONLY_EVALUATE           ?=
ONLY_JUDGE              ?=
SKIP_EVALUATE           ?=
FORCE_JUDGE             ?=
FORCE_CREATE            ?=
INCLUDE_JUDGES          ?=
# Host user identity — passed to docker exec so result files are user-owned
HOST_UID                := $(shell id -u)
HOST_GID                := $(shell id -g)
SCORPION_CONFIG_ID      ?= 925b3c1b-df6f-4a63-89e5-8c3bcfdc3e3f
ORCHESTRATOR_URL        ?= http://localhost:12013/api/v1
CONFIG_SERVICE_URL      ?= http://localhost:12011
EVALUATION_URL          ?= http://localhost:12015/api/v1/evaluate
AUTH_BASE_URL           ?= http://localhost:12000
AUTH_TOKEN              ?=
# Auto-read credentials from .env — no manual input required
BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL    ?= $(shell grep '^BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL=' .env 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2-)
BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD ?= $(shell grep '^BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD=' .env 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2-)

# Legacy aliases (keep old invocations working)
CONFIGURATION_ID        ?= $(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)
METAMODEL_ID            ?=
BENCHMARK_CREATE_OUTPUT ?= evaluation/results/research_created_projects.json
BENCHMARK_EVAL_OUTPUT   ?= evaluation/results/research_evaluations.json
BENCHMARK_FULL_OUTPUT   ?= evaluation/results/research_full_run.json
BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT  ?= evaluation/datasets/ex_01_audit.json
BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR ?= evaluation/results

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target strings by suite
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
_T_FULL      := FullSystem:$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)
_T_BASELINES := Baseline-Direct:direct_llm,Baseline-ReAct:react_agent,Baseline-CodeGen:code_gen
_T_ABLATION  := Ablation-NoMetamodel:ablation:no_metamodel_guide,Ablation-NoBlackboard:ablation:no_blackboard,Ablation-NoRAG:ablation:no_rag
_T_MODIFY    := ModifyVsRegen:modify

ifeq ($(SUITE),baselines)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),only-baselines)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_BASELINES)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),ablation)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_ABLATION)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),only-ablation)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_ABLATION)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),full)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),modify)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_MODIFY)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),smoke)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_MODIFY)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),all)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES),$(_T_ABLATION),$(_T_MODIFY)
else
$(error Unknown SUITE=$(SUITE). Use: baselines | only-baselines | ablation | only-ablation | modify | smoke | full | all)
endif

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared auth macro — acquires a JWT and exports it as $$TOKEN.
# Uses AUTH_TOKEN directly if provided; otherwise logs in from .env credentials.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
define _GET_TOKEN
TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
	if [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" ] || [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL or BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD not set in .env"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  Attempting auth for $(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)..."; \
	RESPONSE=$$(curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12010/api/auth/login/" \
		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}" \
		|| curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12000/api/v1/auth/login/" \
		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}"); \
	TOKEN=$$(echo "$$RESPONSE" | $(PYTHON) -c "import sys, json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('access', ''))" 2>/dev/null); \
	if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: Auth failed. Response from server:"; \
		echo "$$RESPONSE"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  Auth OK"; \
fi
endef

# Same macro for inside a running Docker container (uses internal hostnames)
define _GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER
TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
	TOKEN=$$(docker exec benchmark-env python3 -c \
		"import httpx; r=httpx.post('http://auth-service:12010/api/auth/login/', \
		    json={'email':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)','password':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)'}, \
		    timeout=20); r.raise_for_status(); print(r.json()['access'])"); \
	echo "  Auth OK inside container ($(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL))"; \
fi
endef

# Webapp-only services (auth + config + frontend — no orchestrator/artifacts)
WEBAPP_SERVICES = traefik auth-postgres auth-service config-mongo config-service webapp

.PHONY: help up up-webapp down down-webapp logs logs-webapp status clean \
        benchmark-study benchmark-comparative \
        benchmark-rejudge benchmark-reaggregate benchmark-judge \
        benchmark-report benchmark-auth-check \
        benchmark-create benchmark-evaluate benchmark-run-all \
        benchmark-baselines benchmark-run-server benchmark-run-unified

help:
	@echo "Co-Creation Framework"
	@echo "====================="
	@echo ""
	@echo "Stack management"
	@echo "----------------"
	@echo "  make up             Start full stack (all services)"
	@echo "  make up-webapp      Start minimal stack (traefik + auth + config + webapp)"
	@echo "  make down           Stop all services"
	@echo "  make logs           Tail all service logs"
	@echo "  make status         Show service status"
	@echo "  make clean          Stop all services and remove volumes"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Research benchmarks"
	@echo "-------------------"
	@echo "  make benchmark-study              Full study inside Docker (idempotent)."
	@echo "                                    Loops over all ex_*.json datasets."
	@echo "    SUITE=baselines|ablation|modify|smoke|full|all  (default: baselines)"
	@echo "    DATASET=path/to/ex_N.json          run a single dataset instead"
	@echo "    RUN_NAME=my_run                    fixed dir name for resumable runs"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make benchmark-comparative        Single-dataset run on the host."
	@echo "    BENCHMARK_INPUT=...  RUN_NAME=... SUITE=..."
	@echo "    REJUDGE=1            add new judges to an existing run"
	@echo "    REAGGREGATE=1        recompute scores offline (no LLM calls)"
	@echo "    INCLUDE_JUDGES=j1,j2 subset of judges for reaggregation"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make benchmark-rejudge   RUN_NAME=<run>   (shortcut for REJUDGE=1)"
	@echo "  make benchmark-reaggregate RUN_NAME=<run>  (shortcut for REAGGREGATE=1)"
	@echo "  make benchmark-judge     RUN_NAME=<run>   skip creation, run judge on snapshots"
	@echo "  make benchmark-report    RUN_DIR=evaluation/results/<run>"
	@echo "  make benchmark-auth-check                  verify credentials"
	@echo ""
	@echo "SUITE values: baselines | only-baselines | ablation | only-ablation | modify | smoke | full | all"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Auth: credentials are auto-read from .env (BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL / PASSWORD)."
	@echo "      Override with  AUTH_TOKEN=<jwt>  if you have a pre-obtained token."

# ==============================================================================
# Stack targets
# ==============================================================================

up:
	$(DC) up -d --build

up-webapp:
	$(DC) up -d --build $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

down:
	$(DC) down

down-webapp:
	$(DC) stop $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

logs:
	$(DC) logs -f

logs-webapp:
	$(DC) logs -f $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

status:
	$(DC) ps

clean:
	$(DC) down -v --remove-orphans

# ==============================================================================
# Benchmark: setup and auth check
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-setup:
	@echo "Ensuring benchmarking configuration exists..."
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	curl -s -X GET "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)/api/v1/configurations/$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		-H "Authorization: Bearer $$TOKEN" | grep -q "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" || \
		(echo "  Config $(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID) not found. It should be seeded automatically on config-service startup." && \
		 echo "  If you just started the services, wait a few seconds and try again." && \
		 exit 1)
	@echo "  Benchmarking configuration OK"

benchmark-auth-check:
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	echo "  Token length: $${#TOKEN}"; \
	echo "  benchmark-auth-check: OK"

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-study — main entry point (runs inside Docker, idempotent)
#
# Starts the benchmark container, then launches run_all_benchmarks.sh inside
# it with the chosen SUITE.  Each dataset gets a stable RUN_NAME so re-running
# the same command resumes from where it left off.
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-study: benchmark-setup
	@echo "Starting benchmark container (research profile)..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	echo "Running suite='$(SUITE)' inside container..."; \
	INP="$(DATASET)"; [ -z "$$INP" ] && INP="$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)"; \
	if [ -n "$$INP" ]; then \
		docker exec \
			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
			-e DATASETS_DIR="/dev/null" \
			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
			benchmark-env \
			python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
				--input "$$INP" \
				--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
				--output-dir "/app/src/evaluation/results" \
				$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
				--orchestrator-url "http://langgraph-orchestrator:12013/api/v1" \
				--config-service-url "http://config-service:12011" \
				--evaluation-url "http://evaluation-service:12015/api/v1/evaluate" \
				--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
				--token "$$TOKEN" \
				--auth-base-url "http://auth-service:12010" \
				--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
				--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
				$(if $(REJUDGE),--rejudge,) \
				$(if $(REAGGREGATE),--reaggregate,) \
				$(if $(ONLY_EVALUATE),--only-evaluate,) \
				$(if $(ONLY_JUDGE),--only-judge,) \
				$(if $(SKIP_EVALUATE),--skip-evaluate,) \
				$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
				$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
				$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",); \
	else \
		docker exec \
			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
			-e DATASETS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/datasets" \
			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
			benchmark-env \
			bash /app/src/evaluation/run_all_benchmarks.sh; \
	fi

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-comparative — single dataset, runs on host (fast iteration)
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-comparative: benchmark-setup
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(REJUDGE),--rejudge,) \
		$(if $(REAGGREGATE),--reaggregate,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-rejudge — add new judges to an existing run (no re-creation)
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously started run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-rejudge:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--rejudge \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-reaggregate — recompute scores/kappa from stored judge data (no LLM)
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously evaluated run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-reaggregate:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--reaggregate \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-judge — skip creation, run LLM-as-judge on existing snapshots
# Combines --only-evaluate --rejudge --reaggregate in one step.
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously created run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-judge:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--only-judge \
		$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-report — generate markdown/CSV report from an existing run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-report:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_DIR)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_DIR (e.g., evaluation/results/benchmark_ex_01_audit)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparison_reporter "$(RUN_DIR)"

# ==============================================================================
# Legacy / convenience targets (kept for backwards compatibility)
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-create:
	@if [ -z "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)" ] && [ -z "$(METAMODEL_ID)" ]; then \
		echo "Set CONFIGURATION_ID or METAMODEL_ID"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode create \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_CREATE_OUTPUT)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(CONFIGURATION_ID),--configuration-id "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)",) \
		$(if $(METAMODEL_ID),--metamodel-id "$(METAMODEL_ID)",)

benchmark-evaluate:
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode evaluate \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_EVAL_OUTPUT)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN"

benchmark-run-all:
	@if [ -z "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)" ] && [ -z "$(METAMODEL_ID)" ]; then \
		echo "Set CONFIGURATION_ID or METAMODEL_ID"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode both \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_FULL_OUTPUT)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(CONFIGURATION_ID),--configuration-id "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)",) \
		$(if $(METAMODEL_ID),--metamodel-id "$(METAMODEL_ID)",)

benchmark-baselines:
	@echo "Starting benchmark container (research profile)..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	RUN_TS=$$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S); \
	CONSOLE_LOG="$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)/baselines_console_$${RUN_TS}.log"; \
	mkdir -p "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)"; \
	echo "Running baselines on $(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT)  (log: $$CONSOLE_LOG)"; \
	docker exec \
		-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
		-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
		benchmark-env \
		python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
			--input "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT)" \
			--targets "$(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES)" \
			--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
			--orchestrator-url "http://langgraph-orchestrator:12013/api/v1" \
			--config-service-url "http://config-service:12011" \
			--evaluation-url "http://evaluation-service:12015/api/v1/evaluate" \
			--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			--token "$$TOKEN" \
			--auth-base-url "http://auth-service:12010" \
			--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
	2>&1 | tee "$$CONSOLE_LOG"

benchmark-run-server:
	@echo "Launching server-side benchmark auto-runner (profile: autorun)..."
	@echo "All services must already be up (make up)."
	@echo "Results -> evaluation/results/ (mounted volume)"
	docker compose --profile autorun up --build benchmark-runner

benchmark-run-unified:
	@echo "Starting benchmark container..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	docker exec \
		-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
		-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
		-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
		benchmark-env \
		python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
			--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
			--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
			--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
			$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \

displayContent.text:
SHELL := /bin/bash
DC = docker compose -f docker-compose.yml
PYTHON ?= python3

# ==============================================================================
# Benchmark variables — all can be overridden on the command line
# ==============================================================================
#
# Auth (auto-read from .env, no manual token needed in normal use):
#   AUTH_TOKEN              pre-obtained JWT (optional — login is automatic)
#   BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL    admin e-mail  (read from .env by default)
#   BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD admin password (read from .env by default)
#
# Study scope:
#   SUITE       baselines | ablation | full | all  (default: baselines)
#   DATASET     path to a single ex_*.json; empty = every ex_*.json
#   RUN_NAME    fixed directory name for idempotent / resumable runs
#
# Judge management (for existing runs, no re-creation needed):
#   REJUDGE=1       run only NEW judges not yet in stored results
#   REAGGREGATE=1   recompute scores/kappa offline (no LLM calls)
#   INCLUDE_JUDGES  comma-separated judge names to use in reaggregation
#
# Quick reference:
#   make benchmark-study                          # baselines suite, all datasets
#   make benchmark-study SUITE=ablation           # ablation suite
#   make benchmark-study SUITE=all RUN_NAME=pub   # full study, resumable
#   make benchmark-study DATASET=evaluation/datasets/ex_01_audit.json RUN_NAME=smoke
#   make benchmark-comparative RUN_NAME=smoke     # single-dataset, host-side
#   make benchmark-rejudge    RUN_NAME=smoke       # add new judges to existing run
#   make benchmark-reaggregate RUN_NAME=smoke      # offline score recompute
#   make benchmark-report     RUN_DIR=evaluation/results/smoke

BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR    ?= evaluation/results
BENCHMARK_INPUT         ?= evaluation/datasets/research_input.json
SUITE                   ?= baselines
RUN_NAME                ?=
DATASET                 ?=
REJUDGE                 ?=
REAGGREGATE             ?=
ONLY_EVALUATE           ?=
ONLY_JUDGE              ?=
SKIP_EVALUATE           ?=
FORCE_JUDGE             ?=
FORCE_CREATE            ?=
INCLUDE_JUDGES          ?=
# Host user identity — passed to docker exec so result files are user-owned
HOST_UID                := $(shell id -u)
HOST_GID                := $(shell id -g)
SCORPION_CONFIG_ID      ?= 925b3c1b-df6f-4a63-89e5-8c3bcfdc3e3f
ORCHESTRATOR_URL        ?= http://localhost:12013/api/v1
CONFIG_SERVICE_URL      ?= http://localhost:12011
EVALUATION_URL          ?= http://localhost:12015/api/v1/evaluate
AUTH_BASE_URL           ?= http://localhost:12000
AUTH_TOKEN              ?=
# Auto-read credentials from .env — no manual input required
BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL    ?= $(shell grep '^BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL=' .env 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2-)
BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD ?= $(shell grep '^BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD=' .env 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2-)

# Legacy aliases (keep old invocations working)
CONFIGURATION_ID        ?= $(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)
METAMODEL_ID            ?=
BENCHMARK_CREATE_OUTPUT ?= evaluation/results/research_created_projects.json
BENCHMARK_EVAL_OUTPUT   ?= evaluation/results/research_evaluations.json
BENCHMARK_FULL_OUTPUT   ?= evaluation/results/research_full_run.json
BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT  ?= evaluation/datasets/ex_01_audit.json
BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR ?= evaluation/results

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target strings by suite
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
_T_FULL      := FullSystem:$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)
_T_BASELINES := Baseline-Direct:direct_llm,Baseline-ReAct:react_agent,Baseline-CodeGen:code_gen
_T_ABLATION  := Ablation-NoMetamodel:ablation:no_metamodel_guide,Ablation-NoBlackboard:ablation:no_blackboard,Ablation-NoRAG:ablation:no_rag
_T_MODIFY    := ModifyVsRegen:modify

ifeq ($(SUITE),baselines)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),only-baselines)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_BASELINES)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),ablation)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_ABLATION)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),only-ablation)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_ABLATION)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),full)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),modify)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_MODIFY)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),smoke)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_MODIFY)
else ifeq ($(SUITE),all)
_ACTIVE_TARGETS := $(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES),$(_T_ABLATION),$(_T_MODIFY)
else
$(error Unknown SUITE=$(SUITE). Use: baselines | only-baselines | ablation | only-ablation | modify | smoke | full | all)
endif

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared auth macro — acquires a JWT and exports it as $$TOKEN.
# Uses AUTH_TOKEN directly if provided; otherwise logs in from .env credentials.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
define _GET_TOKEN
TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
	if [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" ] || [ -z "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL or BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD not set in .env"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  Attempting auth for $(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)..."; \
	RESPONSE=$$(curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12010/api/auth/login/" \
		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}" \
		|| curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:12000/api/v1/auth/login/" \
		-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
		-d "{\"email\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)\",\"password\":\"$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)\"}"); \
	TOKEN=$$(echo "$$RESPONSE" | $(PYTHON) -c "import sys, json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('access', ''))" 2>/dev/null); \
	if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: Auth failed. Response from server:"; \
		echo "$$RESPONSE"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  Auth OK"; \
fi
endef

# Same macro for inside a running Docker container (uses internal hostnames)
define _GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER
TOKEN="$(AUTH_TOKEN)"; \
if [ -z "$$TOKEN" ]; then \
	TOKEN=$$(docker exec benchmark-env python3 -c \
		"import httpx; r=httpx.post('http://auth-service:12010/api/auth/login/', \
		    json={'email':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)','password':'$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)'}, \
		    timeout=20); r.raise_for_status(); print(r.json()['access'])"); \
	echo "  Auth OK inside container ($(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL))"; \
fi
endef

# Webapp-only services (auth + config + frontend — no orchestrator/artifacts)
WEBAPP_SERVICES = traefik auth-postgres auth-service config-mongo config-service webapp

.PHONY: help up up-webapp down down-webapp logs logs-webapp status clean \
        benchmark-study benchmark-comparative \
        benchmark-rejudge benchmark-reaggregate benchmark-judge \
        benchmark-report benchmark-auth-check \
        benchmark-create benchmark-evaluate benchmark-run-all \
        benchmark-baselines benchmark-run-server benchmark-run-unified

help:
	@echo "Co-Creation Framework"
	@echo "====================="
	@echo ""
	@echo "Stack management"
	@echo "----------------"
	@echo "  make up             Start full stack (all services)"
	@echo "  make up-webapp      Start minimal stack (traefik + auth + config + webapp)"
	@echo "  make down           Stop all services"
	@echo "  make logs           Tail all service logs"
	@echo "  make status         Show service status"
	@echo "  make clean          Stop all services and remove volumes"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Research benchmarks"
	@echo "-------------------"
	@echo "  make benchmark-study              Full study inside Docker (idempotent)."
	@echo "                                    Loops over all ex_*.json datasets."
	@echo "    SUITE=baselines|ablation|modify|smoke|full|all  (default: baselines)"
	@echo "    DATASET=path/to/ex_N.json          run a single dataset instead"
	@echo "    RUN_NAME=my_run                    fixed dir name for resumable runs"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make benchmark-comparative        Single-dataset run on the host."
	@echo "    BENCHMARK_INPUT=...  RUN_NAME=... SUITE=..."
	@echo "    REJUDGE=1            add new judges to an existing run"
	@echo "    REAGGREGATE=1        recompute scores offline (no LLM calls)"
	@echo "    INCLUDE_JUDGES=j1,j2 subset of judges for reaggregation"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make benchmark-rejudge   RUN_NAME=<run>   (shortcut for REJUDGE=1)"
	@echo "  make benchmark-reaggregate RUN_NAME=<run>  (shortcut for REAGGREGATE=1)"
	@echo "  make benchmark-judge     RUN_NAME=<run>   skip creation, run judge on snapshots"
	@echo "  make benchmark-report    RUN_DIR=evaluation/results/<run>"
	@echo "  make benchmark-auth-check                  verify credentials"
	@echo ""
	@echo "SUITE values: baselines | only-baselines | ablation | only-ablation | modify | smoke | full | all"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Auth: credentials are auto-read from .env (BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL / PASSWORD)."
	@echo "      Override with  AUTH_TOKEN=<jwt>  if you have a pre-obtained token."

# ==============================================================================
# Stack targets
# ==============================================================================

up:
	$(DC) up -d --build

up-webapp:
	$(DC) up -d --build $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

down:
	$(DC) down

down-webapp:
	$(DC) stop $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

logs:
	$(DC) logs -f

logs-webapp:
	$(DC) logs -f $(WEBAPP_SERVICES)

status:
	$(DC) ps

clean:
	$(DC) down -v --remove-orphans

# ==============================================================================
# Benchmark: setup and auth check
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-setup:
	@echo "Ensuring benchmarking configuration exists..."
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	curl -s -X GET "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)/api/v1/configurations/$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		-H "Authorization: Bearer $$TOKEN" | grep -q "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" || \
		(echo "  Config $(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID) not found. It should be seeded automatically on config-service startup." && \
		 echo "  If you just started the services, wait a few seconds and try again." && \
		 exit 1)
	@echo "  Benchmarking configuration OK"

benchmark-auth-check:
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	echo "  Token length: $${#TOKEN}"; \
	echo "  benchmark-auth-check: OK"

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-study — main entry point (runs inside Docker, idempotent)
#
# Starts the benchmark container, then launches run_all_benchmarks.sh inside
# it with the chosen SUITE.  Each dataset gets a stable RUN_NAME so re-running
# the same command resumes from where it left off.
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-study: benchmark-setup
	@echo "Starting benchmark container (research profile)..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	echo "Running suite='$(SUITE)' inside container..."; \
	INP="$(DATASET)"; [ -z "$$INP" ] && INP="$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)"; \
	if [ -n "$$INP" ]; then \
		docker exec \
			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
			-e DATASETS_DIR="/dev/null" \
			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
			benchmark-env \
			python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
				--input "$$INP" \
				--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
				--output-dir "/app/src/evaluation/results" \
				$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
				--orchestrator-url "http://langgraph-orchestrator:12013/api/v1" \
				--config-service-url "http://config-service:12011" \
				--evaluation-url "http://evaluation-service:12015/api/v1/evaluate" \
				--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
				--token "$$TOKEN" \
				--auth-base-url "http://auth-service:12010" \
				--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
				--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
				$(if $(REJUDGE),--rejudge,) \
				$(if $(REAGGREGATE),--reaggregate,) \
				$(if $(ONLY_EVALUATE),--only-evaluate,) \
				$(if $(ONLY_JUDGE),--only-judge,) \
				$(if $(SKIP_EVALUATE),--skip-evaluate,) \
				$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
				$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
				$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",); \
	else \
		docker exec \
			-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD="$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
			-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
			-e SCORPION_CONFIG_ID="$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			-e RESULTS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/results" \
			-e DATASETS_DIR="/app/src/evaluation/datasets" \
			-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
			-e HOST_UID="$(HOST_UID)" \
			-e HOST_GID="$(HOST_GID)" \
			benchmark-env \
			bash /app/src/evaluation/run_all_benchmarks.sh; \
	fi

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-comparative — single dataset, runs on host (fast iteration)
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-comparative: benchmark-setup
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(REJUDGE),--rejudge,) \
		$(if $(REAGGREGATE),--reaggregate,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-rejudge — add new judges to an existing run (no re-creation)
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously started run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-rejudge:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--rejudge \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-reaggregate — recompute scores/kappa from stored judge data (no LLM)
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously evaluated run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-reaggregate:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--reaggregate \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-judge — skip creation, run LLM-as-judge on existing snapshots
# Combines --only-evaluate --rejudge --reaggregate in one step.
# Requires: RUN_NAME pointing to a previously created run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-judge:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_NAME)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_NAME to the existing run directory name"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
		--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
		--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--config-service-url "$(CONFIG_SERVICE_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
		--auth-base-url "$(AUTH_BASE_URL)" \
		--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
		--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		--only-judge \
		$(if $(FORCE_JUDGE),--force-judge,) \
		$(if $(FORCE_CREATE),--force-create,) \
		$(if $(INCLUDE_JUDGES),--include-judges "$(INCLUDE_JUDGES)",)

# ==============================================================================
# benchmark-report — generate markdown/CSV report from an existing run
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-report:
	@if [ -z "$(RUN_DIR)" ]; then \
		echo "ERROR: set RUN_DIR (e.g., evaluation/results/benchmark_ex_01_audit)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.comparison_reporter "$(RUN_DIR)"

# ==============================================================================
# Legacy / convenience targets (kept for backwards compatibility)
# ==============================================================================

benchmark-create:
	@if [ -z "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)" ] && [ -z "$(METAMODEL_ID)" ]; then \
		echo "Set CONFIGURATION_ID or METAMODEL_ID"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode create \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_CREATE_OUTPUT)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(CONFIGURATION_ID),--configuration-id "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)",) \
		$(if $(METAMODEL_ID),--metamodel-id "$(METAMODEL_ID)",)

benchmark-evaluate:
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode evaluate \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_EVAL_OUTPUT)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN"

benchmark-run-all:
	@if [ -z "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)" ] && [ -z "$(METAMODEL_ID)" ]; then \
		echo "Set CONFIGURATION_ID or METAMODEL_ID"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@$(_GET_TOKEN); \
	$(PYTHON) -m evaluation.autonomous_benchmark \
		--mode both \
		--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
		--output "$(BENCHMARK_FULL_OUTPUT)" \
		--orchestrator-url "$(ORCHESTRATOR_URL)" \
		--evaluation-url "$(EVALUATION_URL)" \
		--token "$$TOKEN" \
		$(if $(CONFIGURATION_ID),--configuration-id "$(CONFIGURATION_ID)",) \
		$(if $(METAMODEL_ID),--metamodel-id "$(METAMODEL_ID)",)

benchmark-baselines:
	@echo "Starting benchmark container (research profile)..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	RUN_TS=$$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S); \
	CONSOLE_LOG="$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)/baselines_console_$${RUN_TS}.log"; \
	mkdir -p "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)"; \
	echo "Running baselines on $(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT)  (log: $$CONSOLE_LOG)"; \
	docker exec \
		-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
		-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
		benchmark-env \
		python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
			--input "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_INPUT)" \
			--targets "$(_T_FULL),$(_T_BASELINES)" \
			--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_BASELINES_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
			--orchestrator-url "http://langgraph-orchestrator:12013/api/v1" \
			--config-service-url "http://config-service:12011" \
			--evaluation-url "http://evaluation-service:12015/api/v1/evaluate" \
			--configuration-id "$(SCORPION_CONFIG_ID)" \
			--token "$$TOKEN" \
			--auth-base-url "http://auth-service:12010" \
			--auth-email "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL)" \
			--auth-password "$(BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD)" \
	2>&1 | tee "$$CONSOLE_LOG"

benchmark-run-server:
	@echo "Launching server-side benchmark auto-runner (profile: autorun)..."
	@echo "All services must already be up (make up)."
	@echo "Results -> evaluation/results/ (mounted volume)"
	docker compose --profile autorun up --build benchmark-runner

benchmark-run-unified:
	@echo "Starting benchmark container..."
	@docker compose --profile research up -d benchmark
	@$(_GET_TOKEN_CONTAINER); \
	docker exec \
		-e AUTH_TOKEN="$$TOKEN" \
		-e BENCHMARK_SUITE="$(SUITE)" \
		-e PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
		benchmark-env \
		python3 -m evaluation.comparative_benchmark \
			--input "$(BENCHMARK_INPUT)" \
			--targets "$(_ACTIVE_TARGETS)" \
			--output-dir "$(BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_DIR)" \
			$(if $(RUN_NAME),--run-name "$(RUN_NAME)",) \

content.text:
"""
Comparative Benchmark Runner — Idempotent & Resumable.

Runs a set of exercises against multiple system configurations (targets).
Supports both actual orchestrator API targets, local baselines, and ablation variants.

KEY DESIGN: Each target x exercise is tracked individually. Re-running the
benchmark skips already-completed steps, enabling multi-day execution:
  - Day 1: run creation (slow, GPU-intensive)
  - Day 2: run evaluation (LLM-as-judge, also GPU-intensive)
  - Day 3: regenerate reports from the accumulated results

Run directory structure:
  evaluation/results/<run_name>/
    run_manifest.json                  — targets, exercises, config (written once)
    <TargetName>/
      results.json                     — per-exercise results (updated incrementally)
      logs/
        exercise_<id>_<project>.json   — structured baseline log
"""

import asyncio
import json
import os
import argparse
import time
import sys
import httpx
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional

CURRENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent
SRC_DIR = CURRENT_DIR.parent
if str(SRC_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC_DIR))

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# File-ownership helpers — fix Docker root-owned files on mounted volumes.
# Set HOST_UID / HOST_GID in the environment (or pass via docker exec -e) to
# have every created file/directory chown'd to the host user automatically.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
_HOST_UID: Optional[int] = None
_HOST_GID: Optional[int] = None

def _init_host_ownership() -> None:
    global _HOST_UID, _HOST_GID
    uid_s = os.getenv("HOST_UID", "").strip()
    gid_s = os.getenv("HOST_GID", "").strip()
    if uid_s and gid_s:
        try:
            uid, gid = int(uid_s), int(gid_s)
            if uid > 0 and gid >= 0:
                _HOST_UID, _HOST_GID = uid, gid
        except ValueError:
            pass

_init_host_ownership()


def _fix_ownership(path: Path) -> None:
    """chown *path* to HOST_UID:HOST_GID when running inside Docker as root."""
    if _HOST_UID is None or _HOST_GID is None:
        return
    try:
        os.chown(path, _HOST_UID, _HOST_GID)
    except OSError:
        pass

ORCHESTRATOR_DIR = SRC_DIR / "langgraph-orchestrator"
if str(ORCHESTRATOR_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(ORCHESTRATOR_DIR))

from evaluation.autonomous_benchmark import (
    AutonomousBenchmarkRunner,
    _normalize_dataset_payload,
    _normalize_modification_payload,
    ExerciseInput,
    ModificationExercise,
    ModificationBenchmarkInput,
)
from autonomous.pydantic_loader import load_models_from_file
from autonomous.metamodel_analyzer import MetamodelAnalyzer


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Metamodel loader
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _load_metamodel_from_config_service(
    config_service_url: str,
    configuration_id: str,
    token: str,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    url = f"{config_service_url.rstrip('/')}/api/v1/configurations/{configuration_id}/files/metamodel"
    source = ""
    try:
        resp = httpx.get(url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}, timeout=30)
        resp.raise_for_status()
        source = resp.text
    except Exception as exc:
        print(f"  Could not download metamodel from config-service: {exc}")
        # Local fallback for development/research if the service is not ready
        local_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "assets" / "scorpion-reduced" / "metamodel.py"
        if local_path.exists():
            print(f"  Falling back to local metamodel file: {local_path}")
            source = local_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        else:
            return {}

    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
        mode="w", suffix=".py", delete=False, encoding="utf-8"
    ) as tmp:
        tmp.write(source)
        tmp_path = tmp.name

    try:
        models = load_models_from_file(tmp_path)
        print(f"  Loaded {len(models)} metamodel classes from config-service ({configuration_id[:8]}...)")
        return models
    except Exception as exc:
        print(f"  Could not parse metamodel: {exc}")
        return {}
    finally:
        Path(tmp_path).unlink(missing_ok=True)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target specification parser
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def parse_targets(targets_str: str) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, str]]:
    """
    Parse target specification string.

    Formats:
      "FullSystem:<config_id>"                         -> orchestrator with full features
      "Baseline-Direct:direct_llm"                     -> local baseline
      "Ablation-NoGuide:ablation:no_metamodel_guide"   -> orchestrator with ablation preset
      "ModifyVsRegen:modify"                           -> modification benchmark (modify + regenerate)
    """
    targets: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = {}
    for part in targets_str.split(","):
        if ":" not in part:
            continue
        name, value = part.split(":", 1)
        name, value = name.strip(), value.strip()
        if value in ("direct_llm", "react_agent", "code_gen"):
            targets[name] = {"special": value}
        elif value == "modify":
            targets[name] = {"special": "modify"}
        elif value.startswith("ablation:"):
            preset = value.split(":", 1)[1]
            targets[name] = {"ablation_preset": preset}
        else:
            targets[name] = {"configuration_id": value}
    return targets


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exercise result helpers — step presence checks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _get_active_judge_panel() -> List[str]:
    """Read the currently configured judge names from judges.yaml."""
    yaml_path = Path(__file__).parent / "framework" / "judges.yaml"
    if not yaml_path.exists():
        return []
    try:
        import yaml
        data = yaml.safe_load(yaml_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        return [j["name"] for j in (data.get("judges") or []) if "name" in j]
    except Exception:
        return []


def _has_creation(result: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    return result.get("status") == "completed" and bool(result.get("project_id"))


def _has_validation(result: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    return "validation" in result.get("detailed_metrics", {})


def _has_evaluation(result: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    ev = result.get("evaluation", {})
    return ev.get("status") in ("completed", "failed") and ev.get("overall_score") is not None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main class
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ComparativeBenchmark:
    def __init__(
        self,
        orchestrator_url: str,
        evaluation_url: str,
        token: str,
        output_base_dir: Path,
        configuration_id: Optional[str] = None,
        config_service_url: str = "http://localhost:12011",
        timeout_seconds: int = 600,
        auth_base_url: str = "",
        auth_email: str = "",
        auth_password: str = "",
    ):
        self.orchestrator_url = orchestrator_url.rstrip("/")
        self.evaluation_url = evaluation_url.rstrip("/")
        self.token = token
        self.output_base_dir = output_base_dir
        self.configuration_id = configuration_id
        self.config_service_url = config_service_url
        self.timeout_seconds = timeout_seconds
        self.auth_base_url = auth_base_url.rstrip("/")
        self.auth_email = auth_email
        self.auth_password = auth_password

        if not self.auth_email:
            self.auth_email = os.getenv("BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL", "")
        if not self.auth_password:
            self.auth_password = os.getenv("BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD", "")
        if not self.auth_base_url:
            self.auth_base_url = os.getenv("AUTH_BASE_URL", "http://auth-service:12010").rstrip("/")

        if not self.token and self.auth_email and self.auth_password and self.auth_base_url:
            self._refresh_token()
            if self.token:
                print(f"  Auto-login succeeded for {self.auth_email}")

        if configuration_id:
            self.metamodel_models = _load_metamodel_from_config_service(
                config_service_url, configuration_id, self.token
            )
        else:
            print("  No --configuration-id provided; attempting local metamodel fallback.")
            local_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "assets" / "scorpion-reduced" / "metamodel.py"
            if local_path.exists():
                print(f"  Loaded local metamodel: {local_path}")
                with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode="w", suffix=".py", delete=False, encoding="utf-8") as tmp:
                    tmp.write(local_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                    tmp_path = tmp.name
                try:
                    self.metamodel_models = load_models_from_file(tmp_path)
                finally:
                    Path(tmp_path).unlink(missing_ok=True)
            else:
                print("  Local metamodel file not found.")
                self.metamodel_models = {}

        if self.metamodel_models:
            analyzer = MetamodelAnalyzer(self.metamodel_models)
            self.metamodel_topology_yaml = analyzer.get_dependency_graph_yaml()
        else:
            self.metamodel_topology_yaml = ""

    def _refresh_token(self) -> None:
        if not (self.auth_base_url and self.auth_email and self.auth_password):
            return
        try:
            resp = httpx.post(
                f"{self.auth_base_url}/api/auth/login/",
                json={"email": self.auth_email, "password": self.auth_password},
                timeout=20,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            new_token = resp.json().get("access", "")
            if new_token:
                self.token = new_token
                print("  Token refreshed.")
        except Exception as exc:
            print(f"  Token refresh failed: {exc}")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Run directory & manifest
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _get_or_create_run_dir(
        self,
        run_name: Optional[str],
        targets: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]],
        exercises: List[ExerciseInput],
    ) -> Path:
        if run_name:
            run_dir = self.output_base_dir / run_name
        else:
            timestamp = time.strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
            run_dir = self.output_base_dir / f"comparative_run_{timestamp}"
        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        _fix_ownership(run_dir)

        manifest_path = run_dir / "run_manifest.json"
        if not manifest_path.exists():
            manifest = {
                "created_at": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),
                "targets": {n: c for n, c in targets.items()},
                "exercises": [{"ID": e.ID, "Name": e.Name} for e in exercises],
                "configuration_id": self.configuration_id,
                "metamodel_classes": list(self.metamodel_models.keys()),
            }
            manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(manifest, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
            _fix_ownership(manifest_path)
        return run_dir

    def _load_target_results(self, target_dir: Path) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Load existing results indexed by exercise_id."""
        results_file = target_dir / "results.json"
        if not results_file.exists():
            return {}
        try:
            data = json.loads(results_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            results_list = data.get("creation", {}).get("results", [])
            return {r["exercise_id"]: r for r in results_list if "exercise_id" in r}
        except Exception:
            return {}

    def _save_target_results(
        self,
        target_dir: Path,
        results_by_id: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]],
        evaluations: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> None:
        results_list = list(results_by_id.values())
        completed = sum(1 for r in results_list if r.get("status") == "completed")
        data = {
            "creation": {
                "total_exercises": len(results_list),
                "completed_exercises": completed,
                "results": results_list,
            },
            "evaluations": evaluations or {},
        }
        results_path = target_dir / "results.json"
        results_path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2, default=str), encoding="utf-8")
        _fix_ownership(results_path)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Exercise loading
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def load_exercises_from_dir(self, input_dir: Path) -> List[ExerciseInput]:
        all_exercises: List[ExerciseInput] = []
        json_files = [input_dir] if input_dir.is_file() else sorted(input_dir.glob("*.json"))
        print(f"  Loading exercises from {len(json_files)} source(s) in {input_dir}")
        for json_file in json_files:
            try:
                payload = json.loads(json_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                # Skip modification datasets — they use a different loader
                if isinstance(payload, dict) and payload.get("type") == "modification":
                    continue
                dataset = _normalize_dataset_payload(payload)
                all_exercises.extend(dataset.exercises)
                print(f"    {json_file.name}: {len(dataset.exercises)} exercises")
            except Exception as exc:
                print(f"    Failed to load {json_file.name}: {exc}")
        return all_exercises

    def load_modification_exercises(self, input_dir: Path) -> List[ModificationExercise]:
        """Load modification benchmark exercises."""
        all_mod_exercises: List[ModificationExercise] = []
        if input_dir.is_file():
            json_files = [input_dir]
        else:
            json_files = sorted(input_dir.glob("mod_*.json"))
            # Also check smoke directory if we are looking at a directory
            if not json_files:
                json_files = sorted(input_dir.glob("smoke/smoke_modification.json"))

        if not json_files:
            return all_mod_exercises
            
        print(f"  Loading modification exercises from {len(json_files)} source(s)")
        for json_file in json_files:
            try:
                payload = json.loads(json_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                if isinstance(payload, dict) and payload.get("type") == "modification":
                    dataset = _normalize_modification_payload(payload)
                    all_mod_exercises.extend(dataset.exercises)
                    print(f"    {json_file.name}: {len(dataset.exercises)} modification exercises")
            except Exception as exc:
                # Only log error if it was explicitly intended to be a modification file
                if json_file.name.startswith("mod_") or (isinstance(payload, dict) and payload.get("type") == "modification"):
                    print(f"    Failed to load modification exercise {json_file.name}: {exc}")
        return all_mod_exercises

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Pydantic validation via MCP
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    async def _save_artifact_snapshot(
        self,
        exercise_id: str,
        project_id: str,
        logs_dir: Path,
    ) -> Optional[Path]:
        """
        Fetch the full artifact list for a completed project and write it to disk.

        The snapshot is stored as  logs/artifacts_{exercise_id}_{project_id}.json
        and contains every artifact's raw data exactly as returned by the
        orchestrator.  All post-creation evaluation steps (validation, rejudge)
        load from this file first so they work without a live database — essential
        for academic reproducibility and long-running multi-day benchmark sessions.
        """
        try:
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as c:
                resp = await c.get(
                    f"{self.orchestrator_url}/projects/{project_id}/artifacts",
                    params={"view": "enriched"},
                    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.token}"},
                )
                if resp.status_code != 200:
                    print(
                        f"       Warning: artifact snapshot fetch returned {resp.status_code}",
                        flush=True,
                    )
                    return None
                payload = resp.json()

            snapshot: Dict[str, Any] = {
                "exercise_id": exercise_id,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "snapshot_at": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()),
                "count": payload.get("count", 0),
                # Artifacts are in _llm_view format: {artifact_id, model_name, fields, refs, ...}
                "artifacts": payload.get("artifacts", []),
            }
            path = logs_dir / f"artifacts_{exercise_id}_{project_id}.json"
            path.write_text(json.dumps(snapshot, indent=2, default=str), encoding="utf-8")

                flush=True,

            print(f"       Warning: artifact snapshot failed: {exc}", flush=True)
            return None

    @staticmethod
    def _pick_name(item: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
        """Best-effort display name for an artifact dict."""
        for key in ("name", "common_name", "title", "hostname", "label"):

        return ""

"""
Comparative Benchmark Runner — Idempotent & Resumable.

Runs a set of exercises against multiple system configurations (targets).
Supports both actual orchestrator API targets, local baselines, and ablation variants.

KEY DESIGN: Each target x exercise is tracked individually. Re-running the
benchmark skips already-completed steps, enabling multi-day execution:
  - Day 1: run creation (slow, GPU-intensive)
  - Day 2: run evaluation (LLM-as-judge, also GPU-intensive)
  - Day 3: regenerate reports from the accumulated results

Run directory structure:
  evaluation/results/<run_name>/
    run_manifest.json                  — targets, exercises, config (written once)
    <TargetName>/
      results.json                     — per-exercise results (updated incrementally)
      logs/
        exercise_<id>_<project>.json   — structured baseline log
"""

import asyncio
import json
import os
import argparse
import time
import sys
import httpx
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional

CURRENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent
SRC_DIR = CURRENT_DIR.parent
if str(SRC_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC_DIR))

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# File-ownership helpers — fix Docker root-owned files on mounted volumes.
# Set HOST_UID / HOST_GID in the environment (or pass via docker exec -e) to
# have every created file/directory chown'd to the host user automatically.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
_HOST_UID: Optional[int] = None
_HOST_GID: Optional[int] = None

def _init_host_ownership() -> None:
    global _HOST_UID, _HOST_GID
    uid_s = os.getenv("HOST_UID", "").strip()
    gid_s = os.getenv("HOST_GID", "").strip()
    if uid_s and gid_s:
        try:
            uid, gid = int(uid_s), int(gid_s)
            if uid > 0 and gid >= 0:
                _HOST_UID, _HOST_GID = uid, gid
        except ValueError:
            pass

_init_host_ownership()


def _fix_ownership(path: Path) -> None:
    """chown *path* to HOST_UID:HOST_GID when running inside Docker as root."""
    if _HOST_UID is None or _HOST_GID is None:
        return
    try:
        os.chown(path, _HOST_UID, _HOST_GID)
    except OSError:
        pass

ORCHESTRATOR_DIR = SRC_DIR / "langgraph-orchestrator"
if str(ORCHESTRATOR_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(ORCHESTRATOR_DIR))

from evaluation.autonomous_benchmark import (
    AutonomousBenchmarkRunner,
    _normalize_dataset_payload,
    _normalize_modification_payload,
    ExerciseInput,
    ModificationExercise,
    ModificationBenchmarkInput,
)
from autonomous.pydantic_loader import load_models_from_file
from autonomous.metamodel_analyzer import MetamodelAnalyzer


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Metamodel loader
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _load_metamodel_from_config_service(
    config_service_url: str,
    configuration_id: str,
    token: str,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    url = f"{config_service_url.rstrip('/')}/api/v1/configurations/{configuration_id}/files/metamodel"
    source = ""
    try:
        resp = httpx.get(url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}, timeout=30)
        resp.raise_for_status()
        source = resp.text
    except Exception as exc:
        print(f"  Could not download metamodel from config-service: {exc}")
        # Local fallback for development/research if the service is not ready
        local_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "assets" / "scorpion-reduced" / "metamodel.py"
        if local_path.exists():
            print(f"  Falling back to local metamodel file: {local_path}")
            source = local_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        else:
            return {}

    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
        mode="w", suffix=".py", delete=False, encoding="utf-8"
    ) as tmp:
        tmp.write(source)
        tmp_path = tmp.name

    try:
        models = load_models_from_file(tmp_path)
        print(f"  Loaded {len(models)} metamodel classes from config-service ({configuration_id[:8]}...)")
        return models
    except Exception as exc:
        print(f"  Could not parse metamodel: {exc}")
        return {}
    finally:
        Path(tmp_path).unlink(missing_ok=True)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target specification parser
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def parse_targets(targets_str: str) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, str]]:
    """
    Parse target specification string.

    Formats:
      "FullSystem:<config_id>"                         -> orchestrator with full features
      "Baseline-Direct:direct_llm"                     -> local baseline
      "Ablation-NoGuide:ablation:no_metamodel_guide"   -> orchestrator with ablation preset
      "ModifyVsRegen:modify"                           -> modification benchmark (modify + regenerate)
    """
    targets: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = {}
    for part in targets_str.split(","):
        if ":" not in part:
            continue
        name, value = part.split(":", 1)
        name, value = name.strip(), value.strip()
        if value in ("direct_llm", "react_agent", "code_gen"):
            targets[name] = {"special": value}
        elif value == "modify":
            targets[name] = {"special": "modify"}
        elif value.startswith("ablation:"):
            preset = value.split(":", 1)[1]
            targets[name] = {"ablation_preset": preset}
        else:
            targets[name] = {"configuration_id": value}
    return targets


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exercise result helpers — step presence checks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _get_active_judge_panel() -> List[str]:
    """Read the currently configured judge names from judges.yaml."""
    yaml_path = Path(__file__).parent / "framework" / "judges.yaml"
    if not yaml_path.exists():
        return []
    try:
        import yaml
        data = yaml.safe_load(yaml_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        return [j["name"] for j in (data.get("judges") or []) if "name" in j]
    except Exception:
        return []


def _has_creation(result: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    return result.get("status") == "completed" and bool(result.get("project_id"))


def _has_validation(result: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    return "validation" in result.get("detailed_metrics", {})


def _has_evaluation(result: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    ev = result.get("evaluation", {})
    return ev.get("status") in ("completed", "failed") and ev.get("overall_score") is not None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main class
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ComparativeBenchmark:
    def __init__(
        self,
        orchestrator_url: str,
        evaluation_url: str,
        token: str,
        output_base_dir: Path,
        configuration_id: Optional[str] = None,
        config_service_url: str = "http://localhost:12011",
        timeout_seconds: int = 600,
        auth_base_url: str = "",
        auth_email: str = "",
        auth_password: str = "",
    ):
        self.orchestrator_url = orchestrator_url.rstrip("/")
        self.evaluation_url = evaluation_url.rstrip("/")
        self.token = token
        self.output_base_dir = output_base_dir
        self.configuration_id = configuration_id
        self.config_service_url = config_service_url
        self.timeout_seconds = timeout_seconds
        self.auth_base_url = auth_base_url.rstrip("/")
        self.auth_email = auth_email
        self.auth_password = auth_password

        if not self.auth_email:
            self.auth_email = os.getenv("BENCHMARK_AUTH_EMAIL", "")
        if not self.auth_password:
            self.auth_password = os.getenv("BENCHMARK_AUTH_PASSWORD", "")
        if not self.auth_base_url:
            self.auth_base_url = os.getenv("AUTH_BASE_URL", "http://auth-service:12010").rstrip("/")

        if not self.token and self.auth_email and self.auth_password and self.auth_base_url:
            self._refresh_token()
            if self.token:
                print(f"  Auto-login succeeded for {self.auth_email}")

        if configuration_id:
            self.metamodel_models = _load_metamodel_from_config_service(
                config_service_url, configuration_id, self.token
            )
        else:
            print("  No --configuration-id provided; attempting local metamodel fallback.")
            local_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "assets" / "scorpion-reduced" / "metamodel.py"
            if local_path.exists():
                print(f"  Loaded local metamodel: {local_path}")
                with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode="w", suffix=".py", delete=False, encoding="utf-8") as tmp:
                    tmp.write(local_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                    tmp_path = tmp.name
                try:
                    self.metamodel_models = load_models_from_file(tmp_path)
                finally:
                    Path(tmp_path).unlink(missing_ok=True)
            else:
                print("  Local metamodel file not found.")
                self.metamodel_models = {}

        if self.metamodel_models:
            analyzer = MetamodelAnalyzer(self.metamodel_models)
            self.metamodel_topology_yaml = analyzer.get_dependency_graph_yaml()
        else:
            self.metamodel_topology_yaml = ""

    def _refresh_token(self) -> None:
        if not (self.auth_base_url and self.auth_email and self.auth_password):
            return
        try:
            resp = httpx.post(
                f"{self.auth_base_url}/api/auth/login/",
                json={"email": self.auth_email, "password": self.auth_password},
                timeout=20,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            new_token = resp.json().get("access", "")
            if new_token:
                self.token = new_token
                print("  Token refreshed.")
        except Exception as exc:
            print(f"  Token refresh failed: {exc}")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Run directory & manifest
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _get_or_create_run_dir(
        self,
        run_name: Optional[str],
        targets: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]],
        exercises: List[ExerciseInput],
    ) -> Path:
        if run_name:
            run_dir = self.output_base_dir / run_name
        else:
            timestamp = time.strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
            run_dir = self.output_base_dir / f"comparative_run_{timestamp}"
        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        _fix_ownership(run_dir)

        manifest_path = run_dir / "run_manifest.json"
        if not manifest_path.exists():
            manifest = {
                "created_at": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),
                "targets": {n: c for n, c in targets.items()},
                "exercises": [{"ID": e.ID, "Name": e.Name} for e in exercises],
                "configuration_id": self.configuration_id,
                "metamodel_classes": list(self.metamodel_models.keys()),
            }
            manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(manifest, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
            _fix_ownership(manifest_path)
        return run_dir

    def _load_target_results(self, target_dir: Path) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Load existing results indexed by exercise_id."""
        results_file = target_dir / "results.json"
        if not results_file.exists():
            return {}
        try:
            data = json.loads(results_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            results_list = data.get("creation", {}).get("results", [])
            return {r["exercise_id"]: r for r in results_list if "exercise_id" in r}
        except Exception:
            return {}

    def _save_target_results(
        self,
        target_dir: Path,
        results_by_id: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]],
        evaluations: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> None:
        results_list = list(results_by_id.values())
        completed = sum(1 for r in results_list if r.get("status") == "completed")
        data = {
            "creation": {
                "total_exercises": len(results_list),
                "completed_exercises": completed,
                "results": results_list,
            },
            "evaluations": evaluations or {},
        }
        results_path = target_dir / "results.json"
        results_path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2, default=str), encoding="utf-8")
        _fix_ownership(results_path)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Exercise loading
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def load_exercises_from_dir(self, input_dir: Path) -> List[ExerciseInput]:
        all_exercises: List[ExerciseInput] = []
        json_files = [input_dir] if input_dir.is_file() else sorted(input_dir.glob("*.json"))
        print(f"  Loading exercises from {len(json_files)} source(s) in {input_dir}")
        for json_file in json_files:
            try:
                payload = json.loads(json_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                # Skip modification datasets — they use a different loader
                if isinstance(payload, dict) and payload.get("type") == "modification":
                    continue
                dataset = _normalize_dataset_payload(payload)
                all_exercises.extend(dataset.exercises)
                print(f"    {json_file.name}: {len(dataset.exercises)} exercises")
            except Exception as exc:
                print(f"    Failed to load {json_file.name}: {exc}")
        return all_exercises

    def load_modification_exercises(self, input_dir: Path) -> List[ModificationExercise]:
        """Load modification benchmark exercises."""
        all_mod_exercises: List[ModificationExercise] = []
        if input_dir.is_file():
            json_files = [input_dir]
        else:
            json_files = sorted(input_dir.glob("mod_*.json"))
            # Also check smoke directory if we are looking at a directory
            if not json_files:
                json_files = sorted(input_dir.glob("smoke/smoke_modification.json"))

        if not json_files:
            return all_mod_exercises
            
        print(f"  Loading modification exercises from {len(json_files)} source(s)")
        for json_file in json_files:
            try:
                payload = json.loads(json_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                if isinstance(payload, dict) and payload.get("type") == "modification":
                    dataset = _normalize_modification_payload(payload)
                    all_mod_exercises.extend(dataset.exercises)
                    print(f"    {json_file.name}: {len(dataset.exercises)} modification exercises")
            except Exception as exc:
                # Only log error if it was explicitly intended to be a modification file
                if json_file.name.startswith("mod_") or (isinstance(payload, dict) and payload.get("type") == "modification"):
                    print(f"    Failed to load modification exercise {json_file.name}: {exc}")
        return all_mod_exercises

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Pydantic validation via MCP
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    async def _save_artifact_snapshot(
        self,
        exercise_id: str,
        project_id: str,
        logs_dir: Path,
    ) -> Optional[Path]:
        """
        Fetch the full artifact list for a completed project and write it to disk.

        The snapshot is stored as  logs/artifacts_{exercise_id}_{project_id}.json
        and contains every artifact's raw data exactly as returned by the
        orchestrator.  All post-creation evaluation steps (validation, rejudge)
        load from this file first so they work without a live database — essential
        for academic reproducibility and long-running multi-day benchmark sessions.
        """
        try:
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as c:
                resp = await c.get(
                    f"{self.orchestrator_url}/projects/{project_id}/artifacts",
                    params={"view": "enriched"},
                    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.token}"},
                )
                if resp.status_code != 200:
                    print(
                        f"       Warning: artifact snapshot fetch returned {resp.status_code}",
                        flush=True,
                    )
                    return None
                payload = resp.json()

            snapshot: Dict[str, Any] = {
                "exercise_id": exercise_id,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "snapshot_at": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()),
                "count": payload.get("count", 0),
                # Artifacts are in _llm_view format: {artifact_id, model_name, fields, refs, ...}
                "artifacts": payload.get("artifacts", []),
            }
            path = logs_dir / f"artifacts_{exercise_id}_{project_id}.json"
            path.write_text(json.dumps(snapshot, indent=2, default=str), encoding="utf-8")
            _fix_ownership(path)
            print(
                f"       Artifact snapshot: {path.name} ({snapshot['count']} artifacts)",
                flush=True,
            )
            return path
        except Exception as exc:
            print(f"       Warning: artifact snapshot failed: {exc}", flush=True)
            return None

    @staticmethod
    def _pick_name(item: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
        """Best-effort display name for an artifact dict."""
        for key in ("name", "common_name", "title", "hostname", "label"):
            v = item.get(key)
            if v and isinstance(v, str):
                return v
        return ""

    def _save_baseline_snapshot(
        self,
        exercise_id: str,
        project_id: str,
        artifacts_dict: Dict[str, List[Dict[str, Any]]],
        logs_dir: Path,
    ) -> Optional[Path]:

           unset:  [...validation errors...]}

        {artifact_id, model_name, name} using the MetamodelAnalyzer to identify
        which fields are reference fields.
        """
        from pydantic import ValidationError

        id_lookup: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = {}

                art_id = item.get("id") or item.get("uuid") or ""
                if art_id:
                    id_lookup[art_id] = {
                        "model_name": model_name,
                        "name": self._pick_name(item),
                    }

        ref_field_map: Dict[str, set] = {}

            from autonomous.metamodel_analyzer import MetamodelAnalyzer

                ref_field_map[model_name] = {
